column4: not tone.

column1: Verbose or overly polite prompts simply add more ambiguity and more tokens for the model to evaluate. Short
column2: clear instructions reduce cognitive and computational load. That’s why they work.

column1: Sam Altman has even noted that “please” and “thank you” cost millions a year because those tokens translate to real compute that needs power and creates negative environmental impact--cooling and emissions.
Nice & clear should get the same result as rude & clear.
Clarity helps. Verbosity hurts.";✅ 1 reaction found;2026-04-27T18:43:12.695Z;Yes

column3: United States;unimax.com;https://www.linkedin.com/in/phil-moen-3448b63/;✅ 1 comment found;2025-03-12T15:02:23.065Z;"Tom Arbuthnot. It's great that they added this feature. Many companies will benefit from it. But I might stop short of calling this management. For sure it is a way to get a list of DR numbers in TAC.

column2: Unimax has been providing number management solutions for years
column3: and as the comments below attest
column4: there are many additional features needed to rise to the level of ""management"". Examples:

column1: - See and use numbers from named ranges
column2: not just a big list of numbers

column1: - Reserve numbers for future use or for vanity
- Age numbers out. Avoid re-using a number too soon
- Provide availability and alerts when under a threshold
- “Warning: Only 5% of your numbers are available”
- Any kind of transformation between number formats
- Avoid assigning a conflicting DID due to extension
- e.g. manage numbers into a format such as e.164
If anyone is interested: https://unimax.com/products/numberpro/";✅ 1 reaction found;2026-04-09T15:40:54.690Z;Yes
